action.reasoning:
Observation: TSR's render() passes rays_o[i], rays_d[i] (shape (H,W,3)) directly to renderer. My code flattens which may cause the chunking issue. Also LPIPS is high (~0.60). Let me use exactly the same call pattern as TSR's render, but with custom rays from my c2w.

Analysis: The fix: don't flatten rays, pass (H,W,3) directly to model.renderer. Also use chunk_size=131072 (default working) instead of 8192. Then test camera convention more carefully. Given limited time, let me write a clean predict.py using the exact TSR render pattern but with custom c2w, AND test multiple camera convention variants quickly to find best.

Decision: Rewrite predict.py with proper (H,W,3) ray passing matching TSR.render. Test 3 conventions on val and pick best.
action.command: cd /workspace && python local_eval.py --n_objects 10 --mode world && python local_eval.py --n_objects 10 --mode simple && python local_eval.py --n_objects 10 --mode neg_az && python local_eval.py --n_objects 10 --mode simple_relel
